sections.paragraph:
fut. יִפְשֹׁט-

(1) pr. to expand, to spread out, to extend (Syriac ܦܫܰܛ; Arabic بسط; cogn. to פָּטַשׁ No. 1, 2); always intrans., to spread oneself out; used of hostile troops, 1Ch 14:9, 13 1Ch 14:13of a swarm of locusts, Nah 3:16 followed by עַלּ of the land or people; to rush upon, to attack (in order to take booty), Job 1:17 Jud. 9:33 , 44 Jdg 9:44also followed by אֶל 1Sa 27:8 בְּ 2Ch 25:13 28:18 accus. 1Sa 30:14.

(2) to put off a garment (which is done with opening and unfolding it, as on the other hand a garment is bound together when put on; compare פַּטִּישׁ); followed by an acc., Lev 6:4 16:23 Son 5:3, etc. Absol. פְּשֹׁטָה strip off (garments)! Isa 32:11.

Piel, to cause to put off, i.e. to spoil the slain, 1Sa 31:8 2Sa 23:10 1Ch 10:8.

Hiphil, to cause to put off one’s garments.-(a) followed by an acc. of pers., to strip any one (jem. außiehn, entkleiden), Hos 2:3, .-(b) followed by an acc. of the garment, 1Sa 31:9 Job 22:6.-(c) with two acc., to strip any one of any thing, Gen 37:23 Num 20:26, 28 Num 20:28or an acc. of the thing, and מֵעַל of pers., Mic 3:3 (compare מִמּוּל Mic 2:8); Job 19:9.

(2) to flay victims, Levit. 1:6 2Ch 29:34 35:11 (compare Mic 3:3).

Hithpael, to strip oneself of clothing, 1Sa 18:4.
